segments.text: "Solomon made a marriage alliance[^11] with Pharaoh of Egypt. He married Pharaoh's daughter and brought her to live in the City of David until he finished building his palace, the Lord's Temple, and the walls surrounding Jerusalem.
"In those days, however, the people still sacrificed on the high places because a Temple to honor the Lord hadn't yet been built.
"Solomon showed he loved the Lord by following the instructions of his father David, except that he sacrificed and burned offerings on the high places.
"The king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for it was the leading high place. Solomon presented one thousand burnt offerings on the altar there.
"The Lord appeared to Solomon in a dream at Gibeon. God said to him,
Ask what you want me to give you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: "Solomon replied,

You showed your servant David, my father, great trustworthy love because he lived his life before you with faithfulness, doing what was right and committed to principle. You have continued to show this great trustworthy love by giving him a son to sit on his throne to this day.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: Now, Lord God, you have made me king in place of my father David. But I am like an inexperienced young boy who doesn't know what to do. [^12]
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: I, your servant, am here among your chosen people, a great people that are so many they cannot be counted.
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: So please give me a mind that's receptive so I can rule[^13] your people well, understanding the difference between right and wrong, for who can rule this difficult people of yours?
segments.speaker: Solomon

segments.text: "The Lord considered that what Solomon asked for was good.
"So God told him,
Because you asked for this, and you didn't ask for a long life, or wealth, or the death of your enemies, but instead you asked for understanding to know what is right,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am giving you what you asked for. I am giving you a mind that is wise, with an understanding of what is right, more than anyone else before you or after you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I am also giving you what you did not ask for, wealth and status—so much so that no king will compare to you for the whole of your life.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And if you follow my ways by keeping my laws and my commands, as your father David did, I will give you a long life.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: "Then Solomon woke up and realized he'd had a dream. He went back to Jerusalem, and stood in front of the Ark of the Lord's Agreement and he presented burnt offerings and friendship offerings, and he held a feast for all his officials.
"Later two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him for judgment. [^14]
"One of the women spoke up, saying,
If it please Your Majesty: I share a house with this woman. I had a baby while she was living in the house.
segments.speaker: One of the women

segments.text: Three days after the birth of my baby, this woman also had a baby. We were together; there was no one else in the house, just the two of us.
segments.speaker: One of the women

segments.text: During the night this woman's son died because she rolled over on him.
segments.speaker: One of the women

segments.text: She got up in the middle of the night and took my son from beside me while I was sleeping. She lay him close beside her to cuddle him, and she lay her dead son close beside me.
segments.speaker: One of the women

segments.text: When I got up in the morning to nurse my son I saw that he was dead. When I looked closely at him in the light I realized it wasn't my son.
segments.speaker: One of the women

segments.text: "The other woman argued,
No! My son is the one that's alive. Your son is the one that's dead.
segments.speaker: The other woman

segments.text: The first woman objected,
No! Your son is the one that's dead. My son is the one that's alive.
segments.speaker: The other woman

segments.text: They went on quarrelling in front of the king.
"The king intervened, saying,
So this woman says, ‘My son is the one that's alive. Your son is the one that's dead,’ while the other woman says, ‘No! Your son is the one that's dead. My son is the one that's alive.’
segments.speaker: The other woman

segments.text: Bring me a sword,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: the king ordered. So they brought him a sword.
Cut the child that's alive in two, and give half to one woman and half to another,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: he commanded.
"But the woman whose son was alive had so much love for him as a mother that she cried out to the king,
Please, Your Majesty, give her the boy! Don't kill him!
segments.speaker: The other woman

segments.text: But the other woman said,
He won't be mine or yours—cut him in two!
segments.speaker: The other woman

segments.text: "The king gave his verdict.
Give the child that's alive to the first woman,
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: he ordered.
On no account kill him, for she is his real mother.
segments.speaker: The king

segments.text: "When everyone in Israel heard about the verdict the king had delivered, they had great respect for the king, because they recognized the wisdom God had given him to judge rightly.
